name: Circular Route of the Mallos de Agüero
name_original: Iglesia de Santiago, cueva de Al-Foraz y vuelta a los Mallos de Agüero
description: Explore the impressive Mallos de Agüero, the historic Church of Santiago, and the accessible Al-Foraz Cave on this moderate 4.5-mile hike.
region_intro: Hiking around Agüero offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Pre-Pyrenees in Huesca, Spain. The region is defined by the striking Mallos de Agüero, impressive reddish-hued conglomerate rock formations that rise hundreds of meters high. These vertical structures create a rugged backdrop, with trails traversing varied terrain from valleys to elevated viewpoints. The area also features the Sierra de Santo Domingo mountain range and the Hoya de Huesca. Best hiking trails around Agüero The most popular hiking route is Circular Route of the Mallos de Agüero, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the base of the iconic rock formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mallos de Agüero – Mallos de Agüero loop from Agüero, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail offers varied terrain and expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Refugio La Cochera – Church of Santiago loop from Agüero, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through the landscape to the historic Church of Santiago, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Hiking around Agüero is defined by dramatic rock formations, mountain ranges, and historic landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Agüero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Agüero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Agüero?
A: Agüero offers a diverse range of hiking trails suitable for various fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The komoot community has mapped 19 routes in the area, including 5 easy, 12 moderate, and 2 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Agüero?
A: Yes, Agüero has several easy trails perfect for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio La Cochera – Church of Santiago loop from Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.6 miles (5.8 km) long and leads to the historic Church of Santiago. This route is generally completed in under 2 hours and offers a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: What are the most prominent natural features to see while hiking in Agüero?
A: The most iconic natural features are the spectacular &lt;strong&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/strong&gt;, impressive reddish-hued rock formations that rise hundreds of meters high. Many trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939000414/circular-route-of-the-mallos-de-agueero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route of the Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, explore their base. You can also explore the enormous &lt;strong&gt;Al-Foraz Cave&lt;/strong&gt;, which is embedded within these formations and offers unique views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Agüero?
A: Yes, circular routes are very popular in Agüero. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939000414/circular-route-of-the-mallos-de-agueero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route of the Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.5-mile (7.3 km) trail that takes you around the base of the famous rock formations. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14941513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero – Al-Foraz Cave loop from Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.5 km and also circular.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I visit near the hiking trails?
A: Agüero's trails often lead to or pass by significant landmarks. The 12th-century Romanesque &lt;strong&gt;Church of Santiago&lt;/strong&gt;, a National Monument, is a popular destination, accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio La Cochera – Church of Santiago loop&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find &lt;strong&gt;Marcuello Castle&lt;/strong&gt;, which offers spectacular panoramic views over the landscape and is listed as a highlight &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;here&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Agüero?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic scenery of the Mallos de Agüero, the varied terrain, and the well-maintained paths that offer expansive views of the Pre-Pyrenees. The blend of natural beauty and historical sites is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Agüero?
A: Agüero is a small village, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village, serving as a convenient starting point for many of the hiking trails. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot, especially for popular trailheads.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Agüero?
A: The Pre-Pyrenees region around Agüero is generally pleasant for hiking in spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, but early morning or late afternoon hikes are still enjoyable. Winter offers a different, often quieter experience, though some higher trails might be affected by snow or colder conditions.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas of the Mallos de Agüero?
A: Absolutely. Many trails provide stunning views of the Mallos. For elevated perspectives, consider routes that lead to &lt;strong&gt;Punta Común&lt;/strong&gt; (1,191m), a geodetic vertex that offers comprehensive views of the landscape. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cielo - Panoramic View&lt;/a&gt; is a specific highlight known for its expansive vistas.

Q: Can I access hiking trails in Agüero using public transport?
A: Public transport options directly to Agüero are limited. While there might be infrequent bus services connecting to larger towns in the Hoya de Huesca region, for maximum flexibility and access to various trailheads, traveling by car is generally recommended. This allows you to reach specific starting points more easily.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on the hiking trails around Agüero?
A: The terrain around Agüero is varied, reflecting its location in the Pre-Pyrenees. You'll encounter paths ranging from relatively flat sections through valleys to steeper ascents and descents on mountain trails. The ground often consists of rocky paths, dirt tracks, and sometimes gravel, especially around the conglomerate rock formations of the Mallos. Sturdy hiking footwear is recommended.
tips: If you go through the town center for the elderly, they can lend you the keys to the church of Santiago, a National Monument on the outskirts of the town.
You can get the keys to the church to visit its interior by requesting them at the Agüero senior center.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and returns to the charming village of Agüero. You can find parking within the village, and the route is well-marked with explanatory panels at its beginning.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for hikers of all experience levels?
faqs.answer: Classified as a moderate hike, this route requires good physical condition but features easily accessible paths, making it suitable for most levels. It's a great option for those looking for a rewarding experience without extreme difficulty.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail covers approximately 7.3 km and includes a mix of hiking paths, unpaved sections, and some paved parts. You'll navigate through dry ravines, wind between bushes, and traverse the western face of the Mallos de Agüero, often very close to their vertical walls.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features, including the impressive Mallos de Agüero, the historic Church of Santiago, and the easily accessible Al-Foraz Cave. The trail also begins and ends in the picturesque Agüero village.

faqs.question: Is the Iglesia de Santiago, cueva de Al-Foraz and return to los Mallos de Agüero trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like vultures and the varied terrain. Please ensure your dog is comfortable with moderate hikes and varied surfaces.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike or the Reino de los Mallos area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to access the general Mallos de Agüero area, which is part of the Reino de los Mallos. However, if you wish to enter the Church of Santiago, you can obtain keys from the local senior citizens' residence in Agüero.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular scenery year-round. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. During these seasons, the light also beautifully highlights the changing hues of the Mallos de Agüero.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The impressive rock formations of the Mallos de Agüero provide an ideal habitat for birds of prey. You can often observe dozens of vultures soaring in the sky above the cliffs.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sendero Histórico: Huesca, the Camino Natural de la Hoya de Huesca, and the Pista a San Felices.

faqs.question: Are there any specific recommendations for families hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: The Cueva de Al-Foraz is considered a perfect spot for families, offering wonderful views and an opportunity to explore. The overall moderate difficulty makes it suitable for families with children who are used to hiking, but be prepared for varied terrain.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a day hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing to adapt to changing weather conditions. A camera is also highly recommended for the stunning views.

waypoints.name: Agüero village and the Mallos de Agüero
